question: Design a `ComplexNumber` class with `real` and `imaginary` parts. Overload the binary `+` operator to enable addition of two `ComplexNumber` objects.

test_cases.expected: Default-constructed c6: [uninitialized real] + [uninitialized imaginary]i (values will be garbage and unpredictable)
([uninitialized real] + [uninitialized imaginary]i) + (5.00 + 5.00i) = ([uninitialized real] + 5.00) + ([uninitialized imaginary] + 5.00)i (result will be garbage and unpredictable)

errors: The default constructor `ComplexNumber()` leaves the `real` and `imaginary` member variables uninitialized. If an object is created using this constructor (e.g., `ComplexNumber c;`) and its values are accessed before being explicitly assigned, it will contain garbage values. This leads to undefined behavior and makes the class non-robust for default initialization scenarios, as demonstrated in Test Case 3.
